metadata.source: World Bank Carbon Pricing Dashboard, ICAP ETS Map, national legislation
metadata.description: Carbon pricing schemes eligible for CBAM deductions per Article 26 of EU Regulation 2023/956. Prices are approximate EUR equivalents as of Q1 2026.
metadata.disclaimer: For actual CBAM Article 26 deduction claims, verify scheme eligibility with the latest EU Commission implementing acts. Prices fluctuate; use the price actually paid at the time of production.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: MRV phase began 2024. Aligned with EU ETS design. Trading expected H2 2026.
Post-Brexit successor to EU ETS participation. Own CBAM under development.
Top-up tax on power generators; combined with UK ETS forms effective carbon price.
Norway participates in EU ETS via EEA Agreement. CBAM applies to EEA.
One of the world's oldest carbon taxes. Rate varies by sector. Petroleum sector rate is highest.
Linked to EU ETS since 2020. Covers ~55 installations.
CHF 120/tCO2 since 2022. Exemptions for ETS participants. Revenue redistributed.
Very low rate. Ukraine committed to ETS development under EU Association Agreement.

RS.notes: No carbon pricing. EU accession candidate; ETS development planned under Green Agenda for Western Balkans.

ME.notes: No carbon pricing. EU accession candidate. Aluminium smelter (KAP) is significant exporter.

BA.notes: No carbon pricing. High grid emission factor due to coal dependence. Aluminij Mostar is key aluminium exporter.

MK.notes: No carbon pricing. EU accession candidate.

AL.notes: No carbon pricing. Very low grid EF due to near 100% hydropower. EU accession candidate.

XK.notes: No carbon pricing. Extremely high grid EF due to lignite dependence. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 XK is user-assigned.

MD.notes: No carbon pricing. EU association agreement. High import dependency for electricity.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: World's largest ETS by coverage (~4.5 GtCO2). Cement and aluminium sectors planned for 2026-2027 expansion.
8 sub-national pilots (Beijing, Shanghai, Guangdong, Shenzhen, Tianjin, Hubei, Chongqing, Fujian). Being gradually absorbed into national ETS.
Compliance mechanism replacing PAT scheme. Intensity-based with tradable certificates.
INR 400/tonne of coal (not CO2). Implicit carbon price ~$2/tCO2. Not structured as explicit carbon price.
Phase 3 (2021-2025). Broad coverage but low price. Phase 4 from 2026 with tighter caps.
Voluntary Phase 1 (2023-2025). Mandatory Phase 2 from FY2026. GX Surcharge on fossil fuels also planned.
JPY 289/tCO2. Low rate but broad coverage. Revenue funds green investment.
Regional pilot on Sakhalin Island only. Not applicable to major industrial regions.

RU.notes: EU sanctions complicate CBAM interactions. No national carbon pricing. Sakhalin pilot too limited for CBAM deductions.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: 12 northeastern states. Full auction. Not a national scheme; limited CBAM applicability.
Linked with Quebec. Covers ~80% of CA emissions. Sub-national, so CBAM eligibility uncertain.
Sub-national scheme. High initial prices. Political uncertainty after ballot initiative.

US.notes: No federal carbon price. Sub-national schemes have uncertain CBAM eligibility under Article 26.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Federal backstop at CAD 80/tCO2 (2024), rising CAD 15/yr to CAD 170 by 2030. OBPS for industry.
Linked with California. Covers ~80% of Quebec emissions.
Pioneer carbon tax. Revenue neutral initially, now partially allocated to climate action.

carbon_pricing_schemes.scheme_name: Brazil ETS (SBCE - Sistema Brasileiro de Comercio de Emissoes)
carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Legislation approved December 2024 (PL 182/2024). MRV phase 2026, trading 2027. Covers facilities >25ktCO2/yr.

carbon_pricing_schemes.scheme_name: Mexico Carbon Tax (IEPS on fossil fuels)
carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Tax on fossil fuel sales. Natural gas exempt. Rate varies by fuel, approx MXN 70/tCO2 average.
Pilot phase extended. Full operational phase delayed. Covers facilities >100ktCO2/yr.

EG.notes: No carbon pricing. Major fertiliser exporter (urea, ammonia). Exploring voluntary carbon market.

DZ.notes: No carbon pricing. Gas-rich economy. Ammonia and urea production for export.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Exploring carbon pricing under national climate strategy. No operational scheme.

TN.notes: No carbon pricing. Significant exporter of phosphate fertilisers to EU.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Effective rate much lower due to allowances/offsets. Phase 2 (2026-2030) tightening. Base rate R190/tCO2 but effective rate ~R9-45/tCO2 after allowances.

MZ.notes: No carbon pricing. Mozal aluminium smelter is one of Africa's largest; powered partly by Cahora Bassa hydro.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Repeatedly delayed from 2022. IDR 30,000/tCO2 (~EUR 2). Implementation uncertain.
Mandatory for coal power plants >25MW. Cap-and-tax hybrid. Very low penalty rate.
Domestic carbon credit exchange launched 2025. Full ETS planned for 2028.
Voluntary pilot. Mandatory ETS under consideration. Low participation.
Voluntary carbon credit exchange, not a compliance scheme. Not eligible for CBAM deduction.

PH.notes: No carbon pricing. Exploring carbon credit market.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: TWD 300/tCO2 for direct emissions from facilities >25ktCO2/yr. Collected from 2025. Discounts for SBTi-aligned entities.
Reformed Safeguard Mechanism: declining baselines for facilities >100ktCO2/yr. SMCs tradeable. ACCU price ~AUD 38.

carbon_pricing_schemes.scheme_name: New Zealand Emissions Trading Scheme (NZ ETS)
carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: Broad coverage. NZU price volatile. Agriculture entry postponed. Free allocation for EITE industries.
Tax on liquid fuels only. Natural gas and coal exempt. Real value eroded by inflation.
USD 5/tCO2 for facilities >25MW thermal. Offset mechanism introduced 2022.

carbon_pricing_schemes.scheme_name: Colombia Carbon Tax (Impuesto Nacional al Carbono)
carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: COP 23,500/tCO2 in 2024. Natural gas included since 2019. Offset mechanism available.
Restarted 2018 after 2016 suspension. Very low allowance prices. Over-allocation issues.

AE.notes: No carbon pricing. Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) is major primary aluminium exporter to EU. Exploring internal carbon pricing.

SA.notes: No carbon pricing. SABIC and Ma'aden are major fertiliser/aluminium exporters. Blue hydrogen ambitions.

QA.notes: No carbon pricing. QAFCO/QAFAC are major urea/ammonia producers. Qatar Aluminium (Qatalum) is significant.

BH.notes: No carbon pricing. Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) is one of the world's largest aluminium smelters.

OM.notes: No carbon pricing. Sohar Aluminium is key exporter. Green hydrogen projects under development.

PK.notes: No carbon pricing. Considering carbon tax under climate legislation.

BD.notes: No carbon pricing. Growing steel industry (ship-breaking based).

LK.notes: No carbon pricing. Limited CBAM-relevant exports.

NG.notes: No carbon pricing. Dangote refinery/fertiliser complex may increase CBAM-relevant exports.

KE.notes: No carbon pricing. Very low grid EF due to geothermal/hydro. Limited CBAM-relevant exports.

GH.notes: No carbon pricing. Volta Aluminium Company (VALCO) smelter uses Akosombo hydro.

PE.notes: No carbon pricing. Exploring carbon market mechanisms.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: SGD 25/tCO2 from 2024-2025, rising to SGD 45 in 2026-2027, SGD 50-80 by 2030. Covers facilities >25ktCO2/yr.

SG.notes: High carbon tax rate for Asia. Refining hub.

IL.notes: No carbon pricing. Considering ETS aligned with EU. ICL exports fertilisers.

JO.notes: No carbon pricing. Limited industrial base. Lafarge Holcim cement plant.

carbon_pricing_schemes.notes: EEA member. Aluminium smelting is major emitter. Geothermal/hydro grid.
Applies to liquid fossil fuels. ETS participants exempt from carbon tax on covered emissions.
EEA member. Very few covered installations. Uses Swiss CO2 levy.

BY.notes: No carbon pricing. EU sanctions apply. Belaruskali potash/fertiliser exports historically significant.

GE.notes: No carbon pricing. Low grid EF due to hydropower.

AZ.notes: No carbon pricing. Oil/gas economy. Growing aluminium production.

AM.notes: No carbon pricing. Armenian aluminium foil exports.

UZ.notes: No carbon pricing. Navoi Mining fertiliser production.

ET.notes: No carbon pricing. Very low grid EF (hydropower dominant). Limited industrial exports.

cbam_article_26_rules.description: Rules for deducting carbon prices paid in the country of origin from CBAM certificate obligations
key_requirements: Carbon price must have been effectively paid in the country of origin
No free allocation or rebate was received for the exported goods
The carbon price was not subject to export rebate or compensation
The carbon price applies to greenhouse gas emissions embedded in the imported goods
Only explicit carbon pricing (ETS or carbon tax) is eligible, not implicit carbon costs

calculation.notes: If the carbon price paid in the country of origin exceeds the EU ETS price, no refund is given to the importer.

documentation_required: Proof of carbon price payment (tax receipt or allowance surrender record)
Confirmation that no free allocation was received for exported production
Confirmation that no export rebate was provided

grid_emission_factor_sources.primary: IEA CO2 Emissions from Fuel Combustion (2024 edition)
grid_emission_factor_sources.notes: Grid emission factors represent national average generation-based intensity. For CBAM indirect emissions, use the factor applicable to the specific installation's electricity source where available.
